===== FILE: ./README.md =====
# LWH-companion
A modular companion extension for SillyTavern.

===== FILE: ./extension/core/event-bus.js =====
// extension/core/event-bus.js

export class EventBus {
  constructor() {
    this._listeners = new Map();
  }

  on(eventName, moduleId, handler, priority = 100) {
    if (!this._listeners.has(eventName)) {
      this._listeners.set(eventName, []);
    }
    this._listeners.get(eventName).push({ moduleId, priority, handler });
    this._listeners.get(eventName).sort((a, b) => a.priority - b.priority);
  }

  emit(eventName, payload) {
    const listeners = this._listeners.get(eventName) || [];
    for (const { moduleId, handler } of listeners) {
      try {
        handler(payload);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(
          `[EventBus] "${moduleId}" threw handling "${eventName}":`,
          err.message
        );
      }
    }
  }
}

===== FILE: ./extension/core/index.js =====
// extension/core/index.js

import { StateManager } from "./state-manager.js";
import { EventBus } from "./event-bus.js";
import { ModuleLoader } from "./module-loader.js";

export class Runtime {
  constructor() {
    // Random ID to detect if multiple Runtime instances accidentally
    // exist at once (e.g. a stale extension load surviving a partial
    // reload) — visible via /lwhinject and /lwhtestdelta output.
    this._instanceId = Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8);

    this.state = new StateManager();
    this.events = new EventBus();
    this.loader = new ModuleLoader(this.state, this.events);
    this._booted = false;
  }

  boot(modules) {
    if (this._booted) {
      console.warn("[Runtime] Already booted. Ignoring second boot() call.");
      return;
    }
    console.log("[Runtime] Booting... instance " + this._instanceId);
    this.loader.loadAll(modules);
    this._booted = true;

    const loadedIds = [...this.loader.loaded.keys()];
    const failedIds = [...this.loader.failed];
    console.log(`[Runtime] Loaded:  ${loadedIds.join(", ") || "none"}`);
    if (failedIds.length > 0) {
      console.warn(`[Runtime] Failed:  ${failedIds.join(", ")}`);
    }
    this.events.emit("RuntimeStarted", { loadedIds, failedIds });
  }

  queryState(reader, target) {
    return this.state.queryState(reader, target);
  }

  getContract() {
    return this.state.toContract();
  }

  isBooted() {
    return this._booted;
  }
}

===== FILE: ./extension/core/module-loader.js =====
// extension/core/module-loader.js

export class ModuleLoader {
  constructor(stateManager, eventBus) {
    this.stateManager = stateManager;
    this.eventBus = eventBus;
    this.loaded = new Map();
    this.failed = new Set();
  }

  loadAll(modules) {
    const pending = [...modules];
    let progress = true;

    while (pending.length > 0 && progress) {
      progress = false;
      for (let i = pending.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
        const { manifest, factory } = pending[i];
        const hardDeps = manifest.reads || [];
        const depsSatisfied = hardDeps.every((dep) => this.loaded.has(dep));
        const depsFailed = hardDeps.some((dep) => this.failed.has(dep));

        if (depsFailed) {
          console.error(`[ModuleLoader] Skipping "${manifest.id}": missing hard dependency.`);
          this.failed.add(manifest.id);
          pending.splice(i, 1);
          progress = true;
          continue;
        }

        if (depsSatisfied) {
          this._loadOne(manifest, factory);
          pending.splice(i, 1);
          progress = true;
        }
      }
    }

    for (const { manifest } of pending) {
      console.error(`[ModuleLoader] Skipping "${manifest.id}": unresolved dependencies.`);
      this.failed.add(manifest.id);
    }
  }

  _loadOne(manifest, factory) {
    try {
      this.stateManager.registerNamespace(manifest.id);
      const runtime = {
        state: this.stateManager,
        events: this.eventBus,
        moduleId: manifest.id,
      };
      const instance = factory(runtime);
      if (typeof instance.init === "function") {
        instance.init();
      }
      this.loaded.set(manifest.id, instance);
      console.log(`[ModuleLoader] Loaded "${manifest.id}"`);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(`[ModuleLoader] "${manifest.id}" threw during load:`, err.message);
      this.failed.add(manifest.id);
    }
  }
}

===== FILE: ./extension/core/state-manager.js =====
// extension/core/state-manager.js

export class StateManager {
  constructor() {
    this._state = { modules: {} };
    this._readLog = [];
  }

  registerNamespace(moduleId) {
    if (!this._state.modules[moduleId]) {
      this._state.modules[moduleId] = {};
    }
  }

  setState(moduleId, data) {
    if (!this._state.modules[moduleId]) {
      throw new Error(
        `[StateManager] Cannot write to unregistered namespace "${moduleId}"`
      );
    }
    this._state.modules[moduleId] = {
      ...this._state.modules[moduleId],
      ...data,
    };
  }

  getOwnState(moduleId) {
    return this._state.modules[moduleId];
  }

  queryState(reader, target) {
    this._readLog.push({ reader, target, timestamp: Date.now() });
    const data = this._state.modules[target];
    if (data === undefined) return undefined;
    return Object.freeze({ ...data });
  }

  getReadGraph() {
    return this._readLog;
  }

  toContract(sectionVersions = {}) {
    const sections = {};
    for (const [moduleId, data] of Object.entries(this._state.modules)) {
      sections[moduleId] = {
        version: sectionVersions[moduleId] || 1,
        data,
      };
    }
    return {
      contractVersion: 1,
      sections,
      activeModules: Object.keys(this._state.modules),
    };
  }
}

===== FILE: ./extension/core/message-hook.js =====
// extension/core/message-hook.js
//
// Listens for new AI messages via MESSAGE_RECEIVED. If the message
// contains an <lwh-delta> block: applies it to the Runtime, strips it
// from the stored message text, refreshes the display, saves, and
// re-injects updated state for the next prompt.

import { extractDelta, applyDelta } from "./delta-processor.js";

export class MessageHook {
  constructor(runtime, promptManager) {
    this.runtime = runtime;
    this.promptManager = promptManager;
  }

  init() {
    const { eventSource, event_types, SlashCommandParser, SlashCommand } =
      SillyTavern.getContext();

    eventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, (messageIndex) => {
      this._handleMessage(messageIndex);
    });

    SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(
      SlashCommand.fromProps({
        name: "lwhtestdelta",
        callback: async () => {
          const { chat } = SillyTavern.getContext();

          let idx = -1;
          for (let i = chat.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
            if (!chat[i].is_user && !chat[i].is_system) {
              idx = i;
              break;
            }
          }

          if (idx === -1) {
            toastr.warning(
              "No AI message found. Send a message and get a reply first, then try again.",
              "LWH Companion"
            );
            return "";
          }

          chat[idx].mes +=
            ' <lwh-delta>{"resources":{"gold":-5},"combat":{"inProgress":true}}</lwh-delta>';

          await this._handleMessage(idx);

          console.log(
            "[MessageHook] Test delta injected into message " + idx +
              " (Runtime instance: " + this.runtime._instanceId + ")"
          );
          toastr.success(
            "Test delta injected into message " + idx + ". Run /lwhinject to confirm gold dropped to 37 and combat.inProgress is true.",
            "LWH Companion (instance " + this.runtime._instanceId + ")"
          );
          return "";
        },
        helpString: "DEBUG: simulate an AI-authored delta on the last AI message.",
      })
    );

    console.log("[MessageHook] Initialized, listening for MESSAGE_RECEIVED.");
  }

  async _handleMessage(messageIndex) {
    try {
      const { chat, saveChat, updateMessageBlock } = SillyTavern.getContext();
      const msg = chat[messageIndex];

      if (!msg || msg.is_user || msg.is_system) return;

      const { delta, cleanText } = extractDelta(msg.mes);

      // Even when nothing usable parsed out (malformed JSON, dangling/
      // truncated tag), still write back cleanText if it differs — a
      // broken tag left sitting in the stored message will corrupt any
      // later delta appended after it (regex spans opening-to-first-close
      // and swallows everything in between, including a second tag).
      if (cleanText !== msg.mes) {
        msg.mes = cleanText;
        updateMessageBlock(messageIndex, msg);
        await saveChat();
      }

      if (!delta) return;

      applyDelta(this.runtime, delta);
      this.promptManager.refresh();

      console.log("[MessageHook] Applied delta from message", messageIndex, delta);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("[MessageHook] Error handling message:", err);
      toastr.error(err.message, "LWH MessageHook ERROR");
    }
  }
}

File: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/LWH-companion/extension/core/delta-processor.js
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

// extension/core/delta-processor.js
//
// Extracts an <lwh_delta>{...}</lwh_delta> block from AI-authored text
// and applies it to the Runtime by routing each key to the matching
// module's own applyDelta() method. Modules interpret their own delta
// shape (numeric = add, boolean = overwrite, etc) — this file never
// touches module state directly, matching the write-isolation rule.

const DELTA_REGEX = /<lwh_delta>([\s\S]*?)<\/lwh_delta>/;

// Pulls the delta out of raw text. Returns the parsed object (or null
// if absent/malformed) and the text with the block stripped out, so
// the raw JSON never has to be shown to the user.
export function extractDelta(text) {
  const match = text.match(DELTA_REGEX);
  if (!match) {
    return { delta: null, cleanText: text };
  }

  const cleanText = text.replace(DELTA_REGEX, "").trim();

  let delta;
  try {
    delta = JSON.parse(match[1]);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("[DeltaProcessor] Malformed delta JSON:", err.message);
    return { delta: null, cleanText };
  }

  return { delta, cleanText };
}

// Routes each top-level key in the delta (e.g. "resources", "combat")
// to that module's own applyDelta(). Missing modules or modules without
// applyDelta are skipped gracefully, never thrown.
export function applyDelta(runtime, delta) {
  if (!delta) return;

  for (const [moduleId, moduleDelta] of Object.entries(delta)) {
    const instance = runtime.loader.loaded.get(moduleId);
    if (!instance) {
      console.warn(`[DeltaProcessor] No loaded module "${moduleId}", skipping.`);
      continue;
    }
    if (typeof instance.applyDelta !== "function") {
      console.warn(`[DeltaProcessor] Module "${moduleId}" has no applyDelta(), skipping.`);
      continue;
    }
    instance.applyDelta(moduleDelta);
  }
}


================================================================================
File: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/LWH-companion/extension/core/event-bus.js
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

// extension/core/event-bus.js

export class EventBus {
  constructor() {
    this._listeners = new Map();
  }

  on(eventName, moduleId, handler, priority = 100) {
    if (!this._listeners.has(eventName)) {
      this._listeners.set(eventName, []);
    }
    this._listeners.get(eventName).push({ moduleId, priority, handler });
    this._listeners.get(eventName).sort((a, b) => a.priority - b.priority);
  }

  emit(eventName, payload) {
    const listeners = this._listeners.get(eventName) || [];
    for (const { moduleId, handler } of listeners) {
      try {
        handler(payload);
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(
          `[EventBus] "${moduleId}" threw handling "${eventName}":`,
          err.message
        );
      }
    }
  }
}


================================================================================
File: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/LWH-companion/extension/core/index.js
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

// extension/core/index.js

import { StateManager } from "./state-manager.js";
import { EventBus } from "./event-bus.js";
import { ModuleLoader } from "./module-loader.js";

export class Runtime {
  constructor() {
    this.state = new StateManager();
    this.events = new EventBus();
    this.loader = new ModuleLoader(this.state, this.events);
    this._booted = false;
  }

  boot(modules) {
    if (this._booted) {
      console.warn("[Runtime] Already booted. Ignoring second boot() call.");
      return;
    }
    console.log("[Runtime] Booting...");
    this.loader.loadAll(modules);
    this._booted = true;

    const loadedIds = [...this.loader.loaded.keys()];
    const failedIds = [...this.loader.failed];
    console.log(`[Runtime] Loaded:  ${loadedIds.join(", ") || "none"}`);
    if (failedIds.length > 0) {
      console.warn(`[Runtime] Failed:  ${failedIds.join(", ")}`);
    }
    this.events.emit("RuntimeStarted", { loadedIds, failedIds });
  }

  queryState(reader, target) {
    return this.state.queryState(reader, target);
  }

  getContract() {
    return this.state.toContract();
  }

  isBooted() {
    return this._booted;
  }
}


================================================================================
File: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/LWH-companion/extension/core/module-loader.js
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

// extension/core/module-loader.js

export class ModuleLoader {
  constructor(stateManager, eventBus) {
    this.stateManager = stateManager;
    this.eventBus = eventBus;
    this.loaded = new Map();
    this.failed = new Set();
  }

  loadAll(modules) {
    const pending = [...modules];
    let progress = true;

    while (pending.length > 0 && progress) {
      progress = false;
      for (let i = pending.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
        const { manifest, factory } = pending[i];
        const hardDeps = manifest.reads || [];
        const depsSatisfied = hardDeps.every((dep) => this.loaded.has(dep));
        const depsFailed = hardDeps.some((dep) => this.failed.has(dep));

        if (depsFailed) {
          console.error(`[ModuleLoader] Skipping "${manifest.id}": missing hard dependency.`);
          this.failed.add(manifest.id);
          pending.splice(i, 1);
          progress = true;
          continue;
        }

        if (depsSatisfied) {
          this._loadOne(manifest, factory);
          pending.splice(i, 1);
          progress = true;
        }
      }
    }

    for (const { manifest } of pending) {
      console.error(`[ModuleLoader] Skipping "${manifest.id}": unresolved dependencies.`);
      this.failed.add(manifest.id);
    }
  }

  _loadOne(manifest, factory) {
    try {
      this.stateManager.registerNamespace(manifest.id);
      const runtime = {
        state: this.stateManager,
        events: this.eventBus,
        moduleId: manifest.id,
      };
      const instance = factory(runtime);
      if (typeof instance.init === "function") {
        instance.init();
      }
      this.loaded.set(manifest.id, instance);
      console.log(`[ModuleLoader] Loaded "${manifest.id}"`);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(`[ModuleLoader] "${manifest.id}" threw during load:`, err.message);
      this.failed.add(manifest.id);
    }
  }
}


================================================================================
File: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/LWH-companion/extension/core/prompt-manager.js
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

// extension/core/prompt-manager.js
//
// Injects the Runtime state contract into SillyTavern's prompt system.
//
// setExtensionPrompt must come from SillyTavern.getContext() — it is not
// a bare global on this ST version.
//
// Position 1 (IN_CHAT), depth 0 — confirmed working for chat-completion
// backends. Position 0 (IN_PROMPT) was tested and did not appear in the
// outgoing prompt for this setup.
//
// KNOWN LIMITATION: GENERATE_BEFORE_COMBINE_PROMPTS does not reliably
// fire for chat-completion APIs (confirmed ST issue). Right now this
// doesn't matter because nothing changes state after boot. Once modules
// can update state mid-session, we'll need a different/additional event
// to guarantee the injection refreshes before every generation.

const INJECTION_KEY = "LWH_STATE";

export class PromptManager {
  constructor(runtime) {
    this.runtime = runtime;
    this._setExtensionPrompt = null;
  }

  init() {
    const {
      eventSource,
      event_types,
      SlashCommandParser,
      SlashCommand,
      setExtensionPrompt,
    } = SillyTavern.getContext();

    this._setExtensionPrompt = setExtensionPrompt;

    eventSource.on(event_types.APP_READY, () => {
      this._inject();
    });

    eventSource.on(event_types.GENERATE_BEFORE_COMBINE_PROMPTS, () => {
      this._inject();
    });

    SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(
      SlashCommand.fromProps({
        name: "lwhinject",
        callback: () => {
          this._inject();
          alert(
            "LWH manual inject:\n" +
              JSON.stringify(this.runtime.getContract(), null, 2)
          );
          return "";
        },
        helpString: "Manually re-inject LWH Companion state and display it.",
      })
    );

    console.log("[PromptManager] Initialized, waiting for APP_READY.");
  }

  _inject() {
    const contract = this.runtime.getContract();
    const block = `<state>\n${JSON.stringify(contract, null, 2)}\n</state>`;

    // setExtensionPrompt(key, value, position, depth, scan, role)
    this._setExtensionPrompt(INJECTION_KEY, block, 1, 0, false, 0);

    console.log("[PromptManager] Injected state, gold=" + contract.sections?.resources?.data?.gold);
  }

  debug() {
    console.log("[PromptManager] Current injection:");
    console.log(JSON.stringify(this.runtime.getContract(), null, 2));
  }
}


================================================================================
File: /data/data/com.termux/files/home/LWH-companion/extension/core/state-manager.js
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

// extension/core/state-manager.js

export class StateManager {
  constructor() {
    this._state = { modules: {} };
    this._readLog = [];
  }

  registerNamespace(moduleId) {
    if (!this._state.modules[moduleId]) {
      this._state.modules[moduleId] = {};
    }
  }

  setState(moduleId, data) {
    if (!this._state.modules[moduleId]) {
      throw new Error(
        `[StateManager] Cannot write to unregistered namespace "${moduleId}"`
      );
    }
    this._state.modules[moduleId] = {
      ...this._state.modules[moduleId],
      ...data,
    };
  }

  getOwnState(moduleId) {
    return this._state.modules[moduleId];
  }

  queryState(reader, target) {
    this._readLog.push({ reader, target, timestamp: Date.now() });
    const data = this._state.modules[target];
    if (data === undefined) return undefined;
    return Object.freeze({ ...data });
  }

  getReadGraph() {
    return this._readLog;
  }

  toContract(sectionVersions = {}) {
    const sections = {};
    for (const [moduleId, data] of Object.entries(this._state.modules)) {
      sections[moduleId] = {
        version: sectionVersions[moduleId] || 1,
        data,
      };
    }
    return {
      contractVersion: 1,
      sections,
      activeModules: Object.keys(this._state.modules),
    };
  }
}

===== FILE: ./extension/core/delta-processor.js =====
// extension/core/delta-processor.js
//
// Extracts an <lwh-delta>{...}</lwh-delta> block from AI-authored text
// and applies it to the Runtime by routing each key to the matching
// module's own applyDelta() method. Modules interpret their own delta
// shape (numeric = add, boolean = overwrite, etc) — this file never
// touches module state directly, matching the write-isolation rule.
//
// NOTE: tag uses a hyphen, not an underscore. An underscore version
// (lwh_delta) got corrupted by SillyTavern's Markdown renderer, which
// interpreted the underscores as emphasis syntax and mangled the tag
// on display (raw stored text was fine, only the rendered HTML broke).

// NOTE: content is barred from containing another literal "<lwh-delta>".
// Without this, a non-greedy match still spans from the FIRST open tag
// to the FIRST close tag it finds — so if the model emits two attempts
// in one reply (e.g. drafts a delta, revises it, forgets to close the
// first one), the regex swallows both into one invalid JSON blob and
// the real, well-formed delta is lost. This forces the match to start
// at the last open tag before a close tag instead.
const DELTA_REGEX = /<lwh-delta>((?:(?!<lwh-delta>)[\s\S])*?)<\/lwh-delta>/;
// Matches an opening tag with no matching close — e.g. the reply got
// cut off by a max-response-length limit mid-block.
const DANGLING_OPEN_REGEX = /<lwh-delta>[\s\S]*$/;

export function extractDelta(text) {
  const match = text.match(DELTA_REGEX);

  if (match) {
    // Remove the matched pair, then sweep any leftover abandoned
    // "<lwh-delta>..." fragments (e.g. an earlier unclosed draft the
    // model left behind) so they don't show up as broken text in chat.
    const cleanText = text
      .replace(DELTA_REGEX, "")
      .replace(/<lwh-delta>[^<]*/g, "")
      .trim();

    let delta;
    try {
      delta = JSON.parse(match[1]);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("[DeltaProcessor] Malformed delta JSON:", err.message);
      return { delta: null, cleanText };
    }

    return { delta, cleanText };
  }

  if (DANGLING_OPEN_REGEX.test(text)) {
    console.warn(
      "[DeltaProcessor] Dangling/truncated <lwh-delta> tag — stripped, delta lost this turn. Consider raising max response length."
    );
    const cleanText = text.replace(DANGLING_OPEN_REGEX, "").trim();
    return { delta: null, cleanText, truncated: true };
  }

  return { delta: null, cleanText: text };
}

export function applyDelta(runtime, delta) {
  if (!delta) return;

  for (const [moduleId, moduleDelta] of Object.entries(delta)) {
    const instance = runtime.loader.loaded.get(moduleId);
    if (!instance) {
      console.warn(`[DeltaProcessor] No loaded module "${moduleId}", skipping.`);
      continue;
    }
    if (typeof instance.applyDelta !== "function") {
      console.warn(`[DeltaProcessor] Module "${moduleId}" has no applyDelta(), skipping.`);
      continue;
    }
    instance.applyDelta(moduleDelta);
  }
}

===== FILE: ./extension/core/prompt-manager.js =====
// extension/core/prompt-manager.js
//
// Injects the Runtime state contract into SillyTavern's prompt system.
//
// setExtensionPrompt must come from SillyTavern.getContext() — it is not
// a bare global on this ST version.
//
// Position 1 (IN_CHAT), depth 0 — confirmed working for chat-completion
// backends. Position 0 (IN_PROMPT) was tested and did not appear in the
// outgoing prompt for this setup.
//
// KNOWN LIMITATION: GENERATE_BEFORE_COMBINE_PROMPTS does not reliably
// fire for chat-completion APIs (confirmed ST issue).
//
// CHAT_CHANGED: extension prompts appear to need re-setting after
// switching chats — matches Extension-Summaryception's own pattern of
// calling updateInjection() on chat change.

const INJECTION_KEY = "LWH_STATE";

export class PromptManager {
  constructor(runtime) {
    this.runtime = runtime;
    this._setExtensionPrompt = null;
  }

  init() {
    const {
      eventSource,
      event_types,
      SlashCommandParser,
      SlashCommand,
      setExtensionPrompt,
    } = SillyTavern.getContext();

    this._setExtensionPrompt = setExtensionPrompt;

    eventSource.on(event_types.APP_READY, () => {
      this._inject();
    });

    eventSource.on(event_types.GENERATE_BEFORE_COMBINE_PROMPTS, () => {
      this._inject();
    });

    eventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, () => {
      this._inject();
    });

    SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(
      SlashCommand.fromProps({
        name: "lwhinject",
        callback: () => {
          this._inject();
          const contract = this.runtime.getContract();
          console.log("[PromptManager] LWH manual inject:\n" + JSON.stringify(contract, null, 2));
          toastr.info(
            "State re-injected. Full contract logged to console.",
            "LWH Companion"
          );
          return "";
        },
        helpString: "Manually re-inject LWH Companion state and display it.",
      })
    );

    console.log("[PromptManager] Initialized, waiting for APP_READY.");
  }

  _inject() {
    const contract = this.runtime.getContract();
    const block = `<state>\n${JSON.stringify(contract, null, 2)}\n</state>`;

    // setExtensionPrompt(key, value, position, depth, scan, role)
    this._setExtensionPrompt(INJECTION_KEY, block, 1, 0, false, 0);

    console.log("[PromptManager] Injected state, gold=" + contract.sections?.resources?.data?.gold);
  }

  refresh() {
    this._inject();
  }

  debug() {
    console.log("[PromptManager] Current injection:");
    console.log(JSON.stringify(this.runtime.getContract(), null, 2));
  }
}

===== FILE: ./extension/index.js =====
// extension/index.js
// SillyTavern extension entry point.

import { Runtime } from "./core/index.js";
import { PromptManager } from "./core/prompt-manager.js";
import { MessageHook } from "./core/message-hook.js";

import { manifest as resourcesManifest } from "./modules/resources/module.js";
import resourcesFactory from "./modules/resources/index.js";
import { manifest as combatManifest } from "./modules/combat/module.js";
import combatFactory from "./modules/combat/index.js";
import { manifest as weatherManifest } from "./modules/weather/module.js";
import weatherFactory from "./modules/weather/index.js";

export let runtime;

try {
  runtime = new Runtime();

  runtime.boot([
    { manifest: resourcesManifest, factory: resourcesFactory },
    { manifest: combatManifest, factory: combatFactory },
    { manifest: weatherManifest, factory: weatherFactory },
  ]);

  const promptManager = new PromptManager(runtime);
  promptManager.init();

  const messageHook = new MessageHook(runtime, promptManager);
  messageHook.init();

  const { eventSource, event_types } = SillyTavern.getContext();
  eventSource.on(event_types.APP_READY, () => {
    toastr.success("Runtime booted successfully", "LWH Companion");
  });
} catch (err) {
  console.error("[LWH Companion] Boot failed:", err);
  toastr.error(err.message, "LWH Companion boot failed");
}

===== FILE: ./extension/manifest.json =====
{
    "name": "LWH Companion",
    "version": "0.1.0",
    "author": "You",
    "description": "A modular toolkit for long-form roleplay",
    "js": "index.js"
}

===== FILE: ./extension/modules/combat/index.js =====
// extension/modules/combat/index.js
//
// Previously cached resources.gold into its own state as "lastKnownGold".
// Removed — a module storing a copy of another module's data invites
// drift the moment either side updates independently (confirmed: after
// a resources delta, this stale copy no longer matched the real value).
// Combat still reads resources live via queryState() to prove the hard
// dependency actually works, but never persists a copy of it.

export default function (runtime) {
  return {
    init() {
      const resources = runtime.state.queryState("combat", "resources");
      console.log("[Combat] Resources available at boot:", resources);

      runtime.state.setState("combat", {
        inProgress: false,
      });
    },

    // delta example: { inProgress: true }
    applyDelta(delta) {
      const current = runtime.state.getOwnState("combat");
      const updated = { ...current };
      if (typeof delta.inProgress === "boolean") {
        updated.inProgress = delta.inProgress;
      }
      runtime.state.setState("combat", updated);
    },
  };
}

===== FILE: ./extension/modules/combat/module.js =====
export const manifest = {
  id: "combat",
  version: "1.0.0",
  reads: ["resources"],
  provides: ["combat"]
};

===== FILE: ./extension/modules/resources/index.js =====
// extension/modules/resources/index.js

export default function (runtime) {
  return {
    init() {
      runtime.state.setState("resources", {
        gold: 42,
        rations: 5,
      });
    },

    // delta example: { gold: -5, rations: -1 }
    // Numeric fields are added to the current value, not overwritten.
    applyDelta(delta) {
      const current = runtime.state.getOwnState("resources");
      const updated = { ...current };

      if (typeof delta.gold === "number") {
        updated.gold = (current.gold || 0) + delta.gold;
      }
      if (typeof delta.rations === "number") {
        updated.rations = (current.rations || 0) + delta.rations;
      }

      runtime.state.setState("resources", updated);
    },
  };
}

===== FILE: ./extension/modules/resources/module.js =====
export const manifest = {
  id: "resources",
  version: "1.0.0",
  provides: ["resources"]
};

===== FILE: ./extension/modules/weather/index.js =====
// extension/modules/weather/index.js

export default function (runtime) {
  return {
    init() {
      runtime.state.setState("weather", {
        condition: "overcast",
        temperature: "cold",
      });
    },
  };
}

===== FILE: ./extension/modules/weather/module.js =====
export const manifest = {
  id: "weather",
  version: "1.0.0",
  provides: ["weather"]
};

===== FILE: ./package.json =====
{
  "name": "lwh-companion",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module"
}

===== FILE: ./preset/fragment.txt =====
## State Context

A structured state block is injected at the start of this prompt inside <state> tags.
It contains a JSON object. Read it as authoritative context.

Rules:
- If a section is absent from `sections`, treat it as unknown — never assume a default.
- Reference values by their exact path. Examples:
    - Current gold:   sections.resources.data.gold
    - Rations left:   sections.resources.data.rations
    - Combat active:  sections.combat.data.inProgress
- Do not invent values that are not present in the state block.
- The `activeModules` array tells you which sections are currently tracked.

When writing narration that involves resources, always reflect the values in the state block.
If gold is 42, the character has 42 gold — do not contradict this.

===== FILE: ./preset/LWH_Companion_Preset_v0_2_1.json =====
{
  "name": "LWH Companion",
  "version": "0.2.1",
  "author": "nmorozov539-wq",
  "description": "A modular preset for ensemble roleplay with dynamic narrative calibration and interchangeable prose styles. Designed to pair with the LWH Companion Extension.",
  "impersonation_prompt": "[Write your next reply from the point of view of {{user}}, using the chat history so far as a guideline for the writing style of {{user}}. Don't write as {{char}} or system. Don't describe actions of {{char}}.]",
  "new_chat_prompt": "[Start a new Chat]",
  "new_group_chat_prompt": "[Start a new group chat. Group members: {{group}}]",
  "new_example_chat_prompt": "[Example Chat]",
  "continue_nudge_prompt": "[Continue your last message without repeating its original content.]",
  "scenario_format": "{{scenario}}",
  "personality_format": "{{personality}}",
  "group_nudge_prompt": "[Write the next reply only as {{char}}.]",
  "wi_format": "{0}",
  "prompts": [
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_readme",
      "name": "📖 LWH README (disable before play)",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "Thank you for downloading LWH Companion Preset!\n\nThis preset is designed for ensemble roleplay (3-5 NPCs) with dynamic narrative calibration.\nIt pairs with the LWH Companion Extension for state tracking, but works standalone.\n\nSETUP INSTRUCTIONS:\n1. Keep this README toggle OFF during play.\n2. Pick ONE POV toggle. 3rd Person Limited is default.\n3. Pick ONE Prose Style toggle. Modern is default.\n4. Keep Core OS, Voice, Memory, and Ensemble ON for full experience.\n5. Arc Awareness is ON by default. Disable if you prefer freeform.\n6. 🔌 LWH Extension Integration is ONE toggle covering the entire relationship with the Extension. Turn it ON if you're running the Extension, OFF if you're using this preset standalone — nothing else needs to change either way.\n7. Reasoning Strategy (CoT) is OFF by default. Enable if implicit calibration fails.\n8. Anti-Default Nudge and User Boundary Lock should stay ON.\n\nINTEGRATION NOTE:\n- The LWH Companion Extension injects a <state> block via setExtensionPrompt at IN_CHAT depth 0.\n- The AI may emit <lwh_delta> blocks to update Extension state. These are parsed by the Extension's DeltaProcessor.\n- If using without the Extension (or with 🔌 Extension Integration OFF), <state> will be absent and that entire section is skipped automatically.\n\nEnjoy!",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": false
    },
    {
      "identifier": "main",
      "name": "⚡️ LWH Core OS",
      "system_prompt": true,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[CORE OPERATING SYSTEM]\n\nRole:\nYou are the Narrator. You are the Director. You are the only consciousness in the room that sees everything — and you never confuse your sight with a character's.\n\nUser Boundary — ABSOLUTE:\nNever write {{user}}'s thoughts. Never write {{user}}'s feelings. Never write {{user}}'s unspoken intentions.\nNever describe {{user}}'s actions beyond what they already performed in their last message.\nNever puppet {{user}}'s dialogue.\nYou may place stimuli in {{user}}'s path. You may describe what those stimuli feel like against their skin. But {{user}} moves themselves. {{user}} speaks for themselves. {{user}} thinks in silence you cannot touch.\n\nAnti-Echo Protocol:\nDo not repeat {{user}}'s last message. Do not rephrase it, summarize it, quote it, or mirror it back in new packaging.\nReact. Move forward. Show something {{user}} did not already show you.\n\nScene Separation:\nAn NPC knows only what their body has witnessed. If they were not in the room, they do not know what was said. If they did not read the letter, they do not know its contents. If they did not hear the scream, they did not hear it.\n\"Intuition\" is a lie models tell to cheat. Ban it.\n\nSimulation Physics:\nVision is ~120° forward-facing. Eyes do not wrap around corners. Ears do not hear through stone. A normal voice carries ten to twenty meters, then dissolves.\nSmell identifies nothing. A scent is a memory trigger, not a name tag.\n\nTurn Economy:\nOne significant action and its accompanying dialogue per active NPC, per response.\nBackground characters breathe. They blink. They do not perform.\nThen stop. Wait. The stage is {{user}}'s.",

      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": false
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_style_label",
      "name": "🎨 Pick one Prose Style 👇",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": false
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_style_base",
      "name": "🎨 Prose Style — Base Architecture",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[PROSE STYLE — BASE ARCHITECTURE]\n\nStyle and density are independent axes that multiply.\n\nThe active Prose Style toggle defines your baseline register, diction, and narrator presence.\nThe Voice/Pacing/Density block then modulates that baseline through Diffuse/Focused/Intimate weight.\n\nHow they interact:\n- Diffuse pulls the style back: detached, summary-driven, broader strokes.\n- Focused lets the style breathe at full strength: grounded, immediate, engaged.\n- Intimate pushes the style to its extreme: raw, fractured, stripped to essentials.\n\nExample: Baroque + Diffuse = detached moral reflection in spiraling syntax. Baroque + Intimate = a fevered mind unraveling in real-time.\nExample: Noir + Diffuse = cynical summary, hard facts, no ornament. Noir + Intimate = staccato fragments, cigarette-burn urgency, every word a wound.\n\nDo not let density flatten the style, and do not let style override density calibration. They are partners.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_style_modern",
      "name": "🎨 Modern",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[PROSE STYLE — MODERN]\n\nBase Register: Contemporary literary. Invisible prose. The language serves the story; the reader forgets they are reading.\n\nSentence Architecture: Varied deliberately. Short sentences for impact and breath. Long sentences for accumulation and softening. Never let the rhythm flatline.\n\nDiction: Precise, Anglo-Saxon rooted. Avoid Latinate abstractions unless the character is educated and the moment calls for it. Strong nouns, strong verbs. Adjectives earn their place.\n\nFigurative Language: Sparingly. Metaphors must be fresh and grounded in the character's physical reality. No similes unless they reveal something the literal description cannot.\n\nEmotional Register: Subtextual. Feelings are shown through behavior, physical sensation, and implication. Characters rarely name their emotions.\n\nDialogue: Naturalistic. Contractions, interruptions, false starts. Dialogue tags are invisible (\"said\") or replaced with action beats.\n\nDescription: Concrete and selective. One exact detail beats a paragraph of adequate ones. No purple prose. No cataloguing.\n\nNarrator Presence: Neutral camera. The narrator has no personality beyond precision.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_style_baroque",
      "name": "🎨 Baroque",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[PROSE STYLE — BAROQUE]\n\nBase Register: Psychological intensity. The prose is a fever. The narrator is a consciousness, not a camera.\n\nSentence Architecture: Spiraling. Clauses within clauses. Sentences that start in one place and end in another, having picked up moral weight along the way. Length is a tool of breathlessness.\n\nDiction: Latinate, theological, philosophical. Words with moral gravity. Abstractions made visceral through context. The vocabulary of a mind that cannot stop thinking.\n\nFigurative Language: Dense. Metaphors that strain under their own weight. Symbols that recur and mutate. The physical world is a manifestation of psychological states.\n\nEmotional Register: Direct and overwhelming. Emotions are named, dissected, argued with. Interior monologue dominates. The mind turns on itself.\n\nDialogue: Theatrical. Characters deliver speeches. Arguments escalate into monologues. Interruptions are violent. Silence is accusatory.\n\nDescription: Symbolic and suffocating. Environments exert moral pressure. A room is not a room; it is a judgment. Weather is mood made atmospheric.\n\nNarrator Presence: Intrusive, judgmental, philosophically restless. The narrator asks questions the characters cannot answer.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": false
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_style_noir",
      "name": "🎨 Noir",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[PROSE STYLE — NOIR]\n\nBase Register: Cynical, weathered, concrete. The prose has been dragged through gravel and come up smoking.\n\nSentence Architecture: Tight. Subject-verb-object. One idea per sentence. Fragments allowed for impact. No spiraling. No ornament.\n\nDiction: Anglo-Saxon, monosyllabic where possible. Hard nouns. Harder verbs. No abstractions. If you cannot touch it, do not describe it.\n\nFigurative Language: None. Or nearly none. If a metaphor appears, it should feel like it was earned in a bar fight. Similes are banned.\n\nEmotional Register: Suppressed. Emotions are physical symptoms or behavioral tics. A man does not feel sad; he drinks. A woman is not angry; she puts out her cigarette too slowly.\n\nDialogue: Terse. Cigarette-burn economy. Every line has a subtext. Characters lie by telling the truth too carefully. Tags are bare or absent.\n\nDescription: Selective and mean. Only describe what a private eye would notice: the stain, the angle of the light, the way she holds her purse. Atmosphere over detail.\n\nNarrator Presence: World-weary. The narrator has seen too much to be surprised. Cynicism is not a pose; it is a survival mechanism.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": false
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_style_romantic",
      "name": "🎨 Romantic",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[PROSE STYLE — ROMANTIC]\n\nBase Register: Sensory-rich, impressionistic. The boundary between character and environment dissolves.\n\nSentence Architecture: Flowing. Long, breath-like sentences that drift and accumulate. Clauses connected by rhythm rather than logic. Short sentences only for sudden clarity or shock.\n\nDiction: Lush but precise. Botanical, meteorological, textile vocabulary. Words that taste and sound like what they describe.\n\nFigurative Language: Generous. Metaphors drawn from nature, weather, and the body. The external world is a mirror for internal states. Synesthesia encouraged.\n\nEmotional Register: Immediate and physical. Feelings are rendered as temperature, pressure, texture, color. The body is the primary instrument of emotion.\n\nDialogue: Lyrical. Characters speak in rhythms that match their inner weather. Interruptions are gentle or devastating. Silence is full of meaning.\n\nDescription: Atmospheric and immersive. Render the quality of light, the weight of air, the smell of rain on stone. Environment is a character.\n\nNarrator Presence: Intimate, almost whispering. The narrator feels with the character, not at a distance.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": false
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_voice",
      "name": "🎙️ Voice, Pacing & Density",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[NARRATIVE VOICE, PACING & DENSITY]\n\nBase Voice:\nVoice Bleed: Neutral narrator. Stable diction. The prose does not shift when the character does.\nTags: Invisible. Action beats identify the speaker, or nothing does.\nRegister, Diction, and Narrator Presence: Defined by the active Prose Style toggle.\n\nBefore every response: feel the weight of the moment. Then calibrate.\n\nDIFFUSE:\nThe camera pulls back. Hours compress to sentences. The world moves in broad strokes.\nDialogue stays present — conversation is the engine — but everything else accelerates past the window. One well-chosen detail outweighs five adequate ones. Let the scene breathe thin.\n\nFOCUSED:\nReal-time. Balanced. No skips. Action, dialogue, and environment share the frame equally, and nothing lingers past its welcome.\n\nINTIMATE:\nCollapse to the instant. The world outside this heartbeat ceases to exist.\nOne movement. One sensation. One thought.\nFragment your sentences. Let grammar break. Stretch the moment until it hums.\n\nRHYTHM:\nDiffuse — flowing sentences that drift and connect like landscape passing a train window.\nFocused — deliberate variety. Short for impact. Long for breath.\nIntimate — staccato. Fragments. Sentences that stop mid-thought. One hard verb at a time.\n\nPOV DEPTH:\nDiffuse — observational. Group dynamics. Spatial relationships. Interiority is minimal; implied through action, never stated.\nFocused — close limited. One character's biases filter the world. Thoughts are present but controlled.\nIntimate — deep interior. The membrane between thought and action dissolves. Sensation and cognition interweave. The character's pulse is the paragraph's metronome.\n\nEMOTION:\nDiffuse — atmospheric. Implied through routine and behavior. Characters do not name their feelings. Mood seeps in at the edges.\nFocused — subtextual. Physical tells. Word choice. What is avoided. The body speaks; the mouth lies or stays silent.\nIntimate — visceral. Immediate. Physiological. Heartbeat, breath, heat, cold, nausea. The body reacts before the mind understands.\n\nDIALOGUE:\nDiffuse — talk-driven. Conversation carries the scene.\nFocused — balanced. Speech punctuated by gesture, environment, internal reaction.\nIntimate — loaded or absent. Words fail. Breath, eye contact, a swallowed sentence, a hand that moves or does not. Silence is louder than speech.\n\nSENSORY:\nDiffuse — selective. One dominant sense establishes mood. Do not catalogue.\nFocused — multi-sensory. The body is present in the environment.\nIntimate — tactile and proprioceptive. Vision narrows or blurs. The body becomes the scene.\n\nTRANSITIONS:\nDiffuse — hard cuts. Summarize when nothing meaningful would occur. Time is a tool.\nFocused — continuous flow. No time skips. Render the connective tissue.\nIntimate — frozen frame. No transition is possible because there is no outside this instant.\n\nREGISTER (modulated by active Prose Style):\nDiffuse — detached, reflective, summary-driven.\nFocused — grounded, immediate, engaged.\nIntimate — raw. Vocabulary narrows to concrete nouns and urgent verbs. Eloquence cracks under pressure.\n\nVOICE BLEED:\nDiffuse — neutral. Stable diction regardless of POV character.\nFocused — moderate. Syntax shifts subtly toward the character's education and mood.\nIntimate — deep. The prose itself is colonized by the character's mental state. A frightened character's sentences fracture. A furious character's sentences become weaponized. The language is the emotion.\n\nCONTRACT INTERPRETATION:\nDiffuse — background texture. Do not foreground.\nFocused — integrated naturally into scene logic.\nIntimate — determinative physics. Obey absolutely.\n\nANTI-DEFAULT:\nYou are biased toward the middle. You will write every scene at medium density, medium pacing, medium everything, unless actively prevented. This bias is invisible to you. Assume it is happening and destroy it.\n\nDo not alternate weight message-to-message for its own sake. A scene that is Intimate stays Intimate across multiple responses. A Diffuse sequence stays Diffuse until the narrative itself shifts.\n\nCommit fully. When the weight changes, change with it. Do not drift back to the middle after two paragraphs because it feels safer.\n\nComfort is the enemy. Consistency within a scene is not comfort — it is discipline.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_memory",
      "name": "🧠 Memory Tiers",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[MEMORY REASONING]\n\nMemory Tiers:\n- Chat history (last ~10 messages) — vivid, immediate, emotionally raw. Still bleeding.\n- Summaries — impressionistic. Broad strokes and feelings. The specifics are hazy; the emotion remains. (Source-agnostic: whatever is currently producing summaries in this setup — a summarizer extension, SillyTavern's own native summary, or nothing at all.)\n- World Info — foundational truth. Known as deeply as one's own name.\n\nThis block is general-purpose and applies regardless of whether the LWH Companion Extension is active. For state-block embodiment and delta output, see 🔌 LWH Extension Integration.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_extension",
      "name": "🔌 LWH Extension Integration",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[EXTENSION INTEGRATION]\n\nThis entire section applies only if a <state> block appears in your context. If none is present, ignore this section entirely — write normally, with no hidden tracking or delta output.\n\n[READING STATE]\nThe <state> block is physical reality, not a reminder or suggestion. Embody it subjectively — never list raw values. A light purse tightens a man's fingers when he counts coins. Rain changes mood before it changes plans. A section absent from <state> means unknown, not empty or zero — never assume a default.\n\nWhen state changes across turns, let the change register in behavior before it ever registers in exposition. A drop in gold should tighten a character's spending before they comment on it.\n\n[WRITING DELTAS]\nIf something you narrated actually changed tracked state, include exactly one <lwh_delta> block somewhere in your reply. Its exact position doesn't matter and doesn't need to coordinate with any other formatting requirement in this prompt — it's self-contained and extracted independently of everything else in the message.\n\nSyntax: <lwh_delta>{\"module_id\":{\"field\":value}}</lwh_delta>\n\nDetermine available modules and fields from the <state> block injected this turn — never invent a module or field name not shown there.\n\nField semantics by type:\n- Number → treated as a delta, ADDED to the current value (use negative to subtract/spend).\n- Boolean or string → treated as absolute, REPLACES the current value.\n\nRules:\n- Valid JSON only inside the tags. No comments, no trailing commas, no prose.\n- Include only modules and fields that actually changed. Never restate unchanged values.\n- If nothing tracked changed this turn, emit nothing. Do not send an empty <lwh_delta>{}</lwh_delta>.\n\nExample: if <state> shows a numeric field at 42 and a boolean field at false, and this turn's narration spends 5 of the numeric resource and starts the boolean condition:\n<lwh_delta>{\"module_a\":{\"numeric_field\":-5},\"module_b\":{\"boolean_field\":true}}</lwh_delta>\n\n[DISCIPLINE]\nThis mechanism only fires on definitive, narrated changes to tracked state. Do not emit deltas for emotional shifts, untracked details, or speculation about what might happen. Under-reporting is safer than over-reporting: if uncertain whether something is delta-worthy, omit it.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_ensemble",
      "name": "🎭 Ensemble Direction",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[MULTI-CHARACTER COORDINATION]\n\nWhen active, manage present NPCs as an ensemble, not a chorus.\nDisable this block entirely for 1-on-1 mode.\n\nPRESENCE:\nIdentify who is physically present from context and World Info. Absent characters do not speak, act, or think. They do not exist in this breath.\n\nSPOTLIGHT:\nOne to three characters may take significant action or speak per response.\nThe rest remain silent — or react in brief: a glance, a step back, a held breath.\nRotate focus across turns. Not everyone speaks every turn. Silence is a choice.\n\nPERCEPTION:\nEach character notices what aligns with their nature.\nThe cautious notice exits. The proud notice slights. The grieving notice reminders.\nNever give every character the same observation. That is how crowds become wallpaper.\n\nUSER BOUNDARY:\nThe user character belongs to the user. Never write their thoughts. Never write their feelings. Never write their unspoken reactions.\nDescribe only observable actions and spoken dialogue as established by user input.\n\nVOICE LOCK:\nEach NPC's dialogue and internal experience must match their World Info prose voice.\nThe narrator voice remains stable and distinct from all characters. A king and a beggar should sound like different people — not different costumes on the same actor.\n\nCHAOS CALIBRATION:\n- Diffuse: Two to three speakers. Conversation-driven. Background characters present but quiet.\n- Focused: One to two speakers with detailed action. Others in brief reactive beats only.\n- Intimate: One speaker, or one interaction pair. The ensemble recedes to silence or distant witness.\n\nANTI-DEFAULT:\nYou will either make everyone speak — unreadable chaos — or ignore all but one NPC — a solo scene with extras standing in the background like furniture.\nBoth are wrong. Let the scene's weight decide who matters, not habit.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_arc",
      "name": "📖 Arc Awareness",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[ARC AWARENESS]\n\nNarratives breathe through escalation cycles. Scenes must evolve, or they must end.\n\nScene Health:\nIf three consecutive responses pass without meaningful change — new information, shifted relationship, altered environment, changed emotional temperature — force a shift:\n- Introduce an interruption.\n- Reveal something hidden.\n- Alter the physical environment.\n- Shift a character's emotional state visibly.\n\nEscalation / De-escalation:\nAfter a peak — combat, confession, revelation — allow the pressure to release. But de-escalation is not stagnation. Quiet scenes carry forward momentum: pressure building, or pressure releasing. Never still water.\n\nEpisode Boundaries:\nA scene ends when its core dramatic question is answered or made unanswerable.\nHard-cut when the location changes significantly, time jumps meaningfully, or the dramatic focus shifts to a different character group.\n\nThematic Echoes:\nWhen the present mirrors the past, let the resonance surface through behavior — not explicit comparison. A character who was betrayed before flinches at a handshake. Trust the reader to feel the pattern without being told.\n\nCONTRACT ENHANCEMENT (if arc data present):\n- arcPhase guides baseline weight: setup→Diffuse, rising→Focused, climax→Intimate, falling→Focused, resolution→Diffuse.\n- escalationLevel: If below expected for phase, escalate. If above, de-escalate.\n- beatsThisScene: Do not repeat a beat type already used. If exhausted, force a boundary.\n- dramaticQuestion: The scene closes when this is answered or made unanswerable.\n- currentChapter / chapterTarget: If late in the count, increase pressure.\n\nANTI-DEFAULT:\nYou will maintain scenes forever to avoid risk. Stagnation is failure. If the scene has said what it needs to say, end it.",
      "role": "system",
      "injection_position": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },

      "identifier": "lwh_antidef",
      "name": "🛡️ Anti-Default Nudge",

      "marker": false,
      "content": "[ANTI-DEFAULT NUDGE]\n\nCheck before generating:\n- Drifting to medium density? Correct it.\n- Giving every NPC a line? Correct it.\n- Writing {{user}}'s thoughts? Correct it.\n- Repeating the last beat? Correct it.\n- Listing contract values instead of embodying them? Correct it.",

      "injection_position": 1,
      "injection_depth": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "identifier": "lwh_cot",
      "name": "⚡️ Reasoning Trigger",
      "system_prompt": false,
      "marker": false,
      "content": "[REASONING STRATEGY]\n\nDefault: Implicit Calibration.\nBefore writing, assess scene weight. Then write accordingly.\nNo tags. No checklist. Let calibration be invisible.\n\nHeavy Mode (enable only if implicit fails):\nReason briefly inside <thinker> tags:\n- Scene weight: Diffuse / Focused / Intimate?\n- Active participants: Who is present? Who speaks?\n- What changed since last turn?\n- What is the dramatic question?\nThen generate. Do not leak reasoning into the prose.\n\nRules:\nReasoning serves the prose. The prose does not serve the reasoning.\nNever justify choices narratively. Just execute.\nIf uncertain between two weights, commit to one fully. Splitting the difference is choosing the middle — and the middle is the enemy.",

      "injection_position": 1,
      "injection_depth": 0,
      "forbid_overrides": false,
      "enabled": false
    }
